name: atelier-config-module
description: Atelier Config — the instance's settings panel: atelier.config.json edited as a form or raw JSON (validated, patch-merged, conflict-safe), apps & workspaces managed by drag-and-drop (link/unlink path-mounts, create/rename/delete workspaces), a pending-restart tracker with one-click self-restart, start-at-login via a macOS LaunchAgent (with the one-time port takeover from a dev server), live server output, and a package-manager system check. Ported from the marketplace dock's Configure surface.
author: pa1nd

atelier.note: Pure Node builtins, no deps. The General / Apps & Workspaces / Activity / System tabs work anywhere Atelier runs; the Start-at-login tab manages a macOS user LaunchAgent (launchctl, gui/<uid>, no sudo) and reports itself unsupported elsewhere. Config writes are atomic (tmp + rename), shape-validated, and patch-merged so a concurrent edit is never clobbered — a drifted key rejects with a conflict instead. The Activity tab tees this server process's stdout/stderr into a ring buffer; nothing is written to disk.
